state_variables.name: storedValue
state_variables.semantic_gap: Unlike Ethereum‚ where storedValue is implicitly stored in a memory slot‚ which is allocated by the compiler‚ there is no state storage in Fabric‚ forcing the developer to read/write the state via a predefined string key‚ using the GetState and PutState functions

statements.semantic_gap: This is an implicit write to the state in Ethereum․ In Fabric this has to be an explicit PutState call with the storedValue as the key
In Ethereum‚ reading a state variable is the same as reading a local variable‚ while in Fabric it has to call a GetState API and deserialise the returned byte array

portability_summary.note: SimpleStorage is the simplest case study: only PB-1 is implemented‚ and there are no access control‚ event logging‚ or Ether transfer․ PB-2 through PB-5 are not implemented in this contract
